phrases.french: Un billet pour le Palais du Tau, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.english: One ticket for the Palais du Tau, please.
phrases.phonetic: un bee-YEH poor luh pah-LAY dew TOH, seel voo PLAY
phrases.context: The Palais du Tau was the Archbishop's palace and where kings feasted after their coronation. Now a museum with original cathedral sculptures and coronation treasures.

phrases.french: Où est le talisman de Charlemagne ?
phrases.english: Where is the talisman of Charlemagne?
phrases.phonetic: oo eh luh tah-lees-MON duh shar-luh-MAHN
phrases.context: The museum holds the famous Talisman of Charlemagne — a reliquary pendant said to have contained a fragment of the True Cross, worn around his neck.

phrases.french: On peut réserver la visite des tours ici ?
phrases.english: Can we book the tower visit here?
phrases.phonetic: on puh ray-zair-VAY lah vee-ZEET day TOOR ee-SEE
phrases.context: Tower visits to the cathedral are organised through the Palais du Tau. Book here or in advance online.

tips: A combined ticket covers both the Palais du Tau and cathedral tower access
The Salle du Tau (banquet hall) is where French kings celebrated their coronation feasts — extraordinary Gothic architecture
Don't miss the original cathedral statues on display inside — replaced by copies on the facade due to pollution damage
